app.name: JiraPS
app.one_liner: A PowerShell module to interact with Atlassian Jira via REST APIs, supporting both Cloud (v3) and Data Center/Server deployments with adaptive behavior.

screens.name: PowerShell Console
screens.purpose: Primary interface for executing JiraPS commands and managing Jira resources via PowerShell syntax.

rust_commands.name: ConvertFrom-AtlassianDocumentFormat
rust_commands.purpose: Converts Atlassian Document Format (ADF) objects from Jira Cloud v3 into Markdown; passes plain strings from Data Center unchanged.

rust_commands.name: ConvertTo-AtlassianDocumentFormat
rust_commands.purpose: Converts Markdown input into Atlassian Document Format (ADF) for writing descriptions and comments on Jira Cloud v3.

rust_commands.name: Invoke-JiraMethod
rust_commands.purpose: Executes low-level REST requests to Jira with automatic pagination, rate-limit handling, and Cloud/Data Center adaptation.

rust_commands.name: New-JiraSession
rust_commands.purpose: Establishes an authenticated session with Jira using credentials or custom headers, enabling subsequent API calls.

mvp_plan: week 1: Add server configuration via Set-JiraConfigServer
week 1: Implement core authentication flow with New-JiraSession
week 1: Integrate Invoke-JiraMethod with rate-limit retry logic
week 1: Support basic issue retrieval and transition
acceptance_tests: ConvertTo-AtlassianDocumentFormat correctly transforms Markdown headings, lists, and tables into valid ADF
Invoke-JiraMethod automatically retries on HTTP 429 with exponential backoff and respects Retry-After header
Module auto-detects Jira Cloud vs Data Center and routes API calls appropriately without user intervention
User can authenticate to Jira Cloud using New-JiraSession with credentials and successfully call Get-JiraIssue
